metadata.title: States PCS: Himachal Pradesh GK for HPPSC Exams

questions.question: Central Potato Research Institute is located at which place?
questions.explanation: Central Potato Research Institute is located at Bemloe (Shimla) in Himachal Pradesh

questions.question: Which governor general of India visited Mandi in October 1871?
questions.explanation: Lord Mayo visited Mandi in Himachal Pradesh in October 1871

questions.question: Which Raja of Sirmaur shifted his capital from Rajban to Kalsi?
questions.explanation: The old capital of Sirmaur was Rajban. Udit Prakash shifted his capital from Rajban to Kalsi

questions.question: Who introduced Buddhism in Himachal Pradesh’s Lahaul-Spiti district?
questions.explanation: Padmasambhava, a Buddhist missionary, introduced Buddhism to Lahaul-Spiti in the 8th century. He is credited with spreading Vajrayana Buddhism in the Himalayan regions, including parts of Himachal Pradesh. His visit significantly influenced the religious practices of the Lahaul-Spiti district.

questions.question: Who was the founder of Jaswan state of Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Purab Chand was the founder of Jaswan state of Himachal Pradesh

questions.question: Kala Top Wild life sanctuary is located in which place?
questions.explanation: Kala Top Wild life sanctuary is located in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh

questions.question: Which ancient tribe is considered the earliest known inhabitant of present-day Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Dasas or Dasyus, referenced in the Rigveda, were among the earliest native tribes to inhabit the region now known as Himachal Pradesh, predating Indo-Aryan settlements.

questions.question: Which Mauryan emperor expanded his rule over the Himachal region during the 3rd century BCE?
questions.explanation: Ashoka extended Mauryan control into the Himachal region and spread Buddhism and Mauryan art there after the Kalinga War.

questions.question: Which city served as the capital of the ancient Trigarta kingdom, situated partly in present-day Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Trigarta’s capital was Jalandhar, a region now in Punjab and Himachal Pradesh, as mentioned in ancient texts like the Mahabharata.

questions.question: Who was the Rajput ruler who established the princely state of Chamba in the 6th century CE?
questions.explanation: Raja Maru founded Chamba in the 6th century CE, making it one of Himachal Pradesh’s oldest princely states.
